title: ophrys cornuta subsp. heldreichii
intro: Ophrys cornuta subsp. heldreichii, also known as the Heldreich's bee orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its distinctive bee-like appearance to attract pollinators. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its potential to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits associated with Ophrys cornuta subsp. heldreichii include relief from coughs and colds, reduced fever, and improved respiratory function. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to the lack of scientific research on its safety and efficacy.
